content:
Match List I with List II.

List I (Mixture) List II (Property use to distinguish)
A. 1-butyne, 2-butyne I. Zinc dust distillation
B. $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6, \mathrm{CH}_2=\mathrm{CH}_2$ II. Tollen's reagent
C. $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6, \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{14}$ III. Baeyer's reagent
D. Benzene, phenol IV. Shooty flame test

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
explanation:
Mixture A: 1‑butyne and 2‑butyne

 • The key difference is that 1‑butyne is a terminal alkyne (it has a hydrogen attached to the sp‐carbon) while 2‑butyne is an internal alkyne.

 • Terminal alkynes can react with ammoniacal silver nitrate (the basis of Tollen’s reagent) to form an insoluble silver acetylide.

 • Thus, adding Tollen’s reagent (II) will distinguish 1‑butyne from 2‑butyne.

Mixture B: Benzene and CH₂=CH₂

 • Benzene is aromatic and quite stable, whereas ethylene (CH₂=CH₂) is an alkene with a reactive double bond.

 • Baeyer’s reagent (a cold alkaline KMnO₄ solution, III) rapidly oxidizes alkenes like ethylene (decolorizing the purple solution) but leaves benzene unaffected.

 • So, mixture B is best distinguished using Baeyer’s reagent (III).

Mixture C: C₆H₆ and C₆H₁₄

 • Here we have benzene (an aromatic compound) and hexane (a saturated alkane).

 • Aromatic compounds tend to burn with a sooty (smoky) flame because of their high carbon content and unsaturation, while alkanes burn with a non‐sooty, luminous flame.

 • Therefore, the “shooty flame test” (IV) can differentiate benzene from hexane.

Mixture D: Benzene and Phenol

 • Both are aromatic, but phenol possesses an –OH group, which greatly increases its boiling point compared to benzene.

 • In the presence of zinc dust, a specific distillation technique (I) can exploit this difference; benzene distills over while phenol (which complexes with zinc) does not.

 • Hence, zinc dust distillation (I) is used to differentiate benzene from phenol.

Matching these results:

 A – Tollen’s reagent (II)

 B – Baeyer’s reagent (III)

 C – Shooty flame test (IV)

 D – Zinc dust distillation (I)

Looking at the options, Option A corresponds to:

 A-II, B-III, C-IV, D-I

Thus, the correct answer is Option A.
